title: Sixty-five years ago
summary: Two old men play chess in the park. One of them remembers a small thing about the other from when they were ten — and the other learns who his friend has been all along.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Your turn.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: I know.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: It is your turn for ten minutes now.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: I am thinking.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: You are thinking, or you are sleeping with your eyes open?

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Both.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: If you move that horse, I take it.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: I will not move that horse.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Then move something.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Mr Bao, do you remember when we were ten?

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: What kind of question is that during a chess game?

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: A real question. When we were ten. In school.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: We are playing chess. Move.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: You sat behind me in class. You copied my answers.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: I did not copy. I looked. Looking is not copying.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: I let you look. You always thanked me with a piece of fruit. Sometimes a banana. Once a mango.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Sixty-five years ago.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Yes.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Why do you bring it up today?

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Because I cannot move my horse, and I am thinking about other things.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: I cannot move. It is your turn.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Oh. Yes. Sorry.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Mr Quy?

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Yes?

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: When we were ten, I did not know you were my friend.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: I knew.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: How?

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Friends share fruit. People who are not friends do not.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Move.

body.speaker: Mr Quy
body.text: Yes. Here. I move the small one in front. Not the horse.

body.speaker: Mr Bao
body.text: Good.

grammarNotes.title: Talking about a past time: 'when we were ten'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để đặt khung thời gian trong quá khứ, dùng 'when + chủ ngữ + động từ quá khứ'. Trong bài: 'When we were ten, I did not know you were my friend.' (Khi chúng ta mười tuổi, tôi không biết anh là bạn tôi.) Mệnh đề chính cũng ở quá khứ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To set a past time frame, use 'when + subject + past verb'. From the dialogue: 'When we were ten, I did not know you were my friend.' The main clause is also in the past.

blanks.explanation: 'Your turn.' — Mr Bao is reminding him to move.
'It is your turn for ten minutes now.'
Mr Quy says he is thinking — even if Mr Bao suspects he is sleeping.
Move that horse — a chess piece.
'Do you remember when we were ten?'
When we were ten. In school.
Mr Quy says Mr Bao copied his answers.
Mr Bao thanked him with a piece of fruit.
Sixty-five years ago — they were ten then, they are seventy-five now.
'I did not know you were my friend.'
'Friends share fruit. People who are not friends do not.'

translationVi:
Bác Bảo: Đến lượt anh.
Bác Quý: Tôi biết.
Bác Bảo: Đã đến lượt anh suốt mười phút rồi đấy.
Bác Quý: Tôi đang nghĩ.
Bác Bảo: Anh đang nghĩ, hay anh đang ngủ mở mắt?
Bác Quý: Cả hai.
Bác Bảo: Nếu anh đi con mã đó, tôi ăn ngay.
Bác Quý: Tôi không đi con mã đó.
Bác Bảo: Vậy đi cái gì đi.
Bác Quý: Bác Bảo, bác có nhớ hồi chúng ta mười tuổi không?
Bác Bảo: Câu hỏi gì thế giữa ván cờ?
Bác Quý: Câu hỏi thật. Hồi chúng ta mười tuổi. Trong trường.
Bác Bảo: Mình đang chơi cờ. Đi đi.
Bác Quý: Anh ngồi sau tôi trong lớp. Anh chép bài tôi.
Bác Bảo: Tôi không chép. Tôi nhìn. Nhìn không phải là chép.
Bác Quý: Tôi cho anh nhìn. Anh lúc nào cũng cảm ơn tôi bằng một miếng trái cây. Có khi một quả chuối. Một lần một quả xoài.
Bác Bảo: Sáu mươi lăm năm trước rồi.
Bác Quý: Vâng.
Bác Bảo: Sao hôm nay anh lại nhắc lại?
Bác Quý: Vì tôi không đi được con mã, và tôi đang nghĩ chuyện khác.
Bác Bảo: Tôi không đi được. Đến lượt anh mà.
Bác Quý: Ờ. Phải. Xin lỗi.
Bác Bảo: Bác Quý này?
Bác Quý: Sao?
Bác Bảo: Hồi mười tuổi, tôi không biết anh là bạn tôi.
Bác Quý: Tôi biết.
Bác Bảo: Sao biết?
Bác Quý: Bạn thì chia trái cây. Người không phải bạn thì không.
Bác Bảo: Đi đi.
Bác Quý: Vâng. Đây. Tôi đi quân nhỏ phía trước. Không phải con mã.
Bác Bảo: Tốt.

questions.prompt: What are the two men doing?
questions.explanation: The whole conversation happens during a chess game.

questions.prompt: How long has it been Mr Quy's turn?
questions.explanation: 'It is your turn for ten minutes now.'
questions.hint: Look at Mr Bao's third turn.

questions.prompt: Where does Mr Quy say they sat in school?
options: Mr Bao sat behind Mr Quy in class
questions.hint: Look at Mr Quy's turn about school.

questions.prompt: How does Mr Bao describe what he did with Mr Quy's answers?
options: He looked — and says looking is not copying
questions.explanation: 'I did not copy. I looked. Looking is not copying.'

questions.prompt: What did Mr Bao always do as a thank you?
options: Gave Mr Quy a piece of fruit — sometimes a banana, once a mango
questions.explanation: 'You always thanked me with a piece of fruit. Sometimes a banana. Once a mango.'
questions.hint: Look at Mr Quy's turn about the fruit.
Look at Mr Bao's short reply about time.

questions.prompt: Why does Mr Quy bring this up today?
options: He cannot move his horse, so he is thinking about other things
questions.explanation: 'Because I cannot move my horse, and I am thinking about other things.'
questions.hint: Look at Mr Quy's reply about why.

questions.prompt: What does Mr Bao admit, after a pause?
options: When they were ten, he did not know Mr Quy was his friend
questions.explanation: 'When we were ten, I did not know you were my friend.'
questions.hint: Look at Mr Bao's turn after 'Sorry'.

questions.prompt: How did Mr Quy know they were friends?
options: Friends share fruit; people who are not friends do not
questions.explanation: 'Friends share fruit. People who are not friends do not.'
questions.hint: Look at Mr Quy's reply to 'How?'

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the conversation?
options: Warm and a bit teasing — old friends with a long memory
questions.explanation: The chess game frames a small, late-life recognition between two old friends.
questions.hint: Notice how each speaker handles the other.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Quy says he knew they were friends because 'friends share fruit'. Think of a small action by someone in your life that quietly told you they were on your side. Did you know it at the time, or only later?
